a: Pizza for every dinner for a month
b: Never eat pizza again, ever

a: A pet dragon the size of a cat
b: A pet cat the size of a dragon

a: A family pet that talks but only complains
b: A pet that's silent but clearly judging you

a: Every car journey is a sing-along
b: Every car journey is total silence

a: Kids pick the holiday, parents pick the food
b: Parents pick the holiday, kids pick the food

a: A birthday every month but no presents
b: One birthday a year with double the presents

a: A treehouse that's bigger on the inside
b: A bedroom with a slide down to the kitchen

a: Guaranteed snow every Christmas
b: One extra week of summer holidays each year

a: The dog fetches anything you name, once a day
b: The cat answers one yes/no question a day, honestly

a: Family game night that never ends in a row
b: Family game night with absurdly high stakes

a: Breakfast for dinner twice a week
b: No cereal in the house ever again

a: Grandma's cooking on tap, but she watches every bite
b: A robot chef that's brilliant but a bit smug

a: Your parents can read your texts
b: You can read your parents' texts

a: A holiday anywhere, but you all share one room
b: A holiday close to home with a room each

a: The trampoline is also your bed
b: The bathtub is also a small pool

a: A time machine that only reaches last weekend
b: A teleporter that only reaches the corner shop

a: Family photos where everyone looks amazing but no one remembers the day
b: Blurry photos of days you'll never forget

a: Your room tidies itself but hides everything
b: Your room stays messy but you always find things

a: School starts at 10am, finishes at 5pm
b: School starts at 7am, finishes at 1pm

a: The family group chat is muted forever
b: The family group chat replies for you automatically

a: Unlimited ice cream that melts in 30 seconds
b: One ice cream a week that never melts

a: Pancakes that flip themselves
b: Toast that's always perfectly done

a: Pause time for everyone but you, ten minutes a day
b: Rewind your own day, once a week

a: A garden that grows sweets
b: A garden that grows money, very slowly

a: The family dog ages backwards
b: The family cat genuinely has nine lives

a: You can talk to babies, but they only gossip
b: You can talk to pets, but they only nag

a: You pick the film but sit at the back
b: Someone else picks but you get the best seat and snacks

a: A magic backpack that holds anything
b: Shoes that make you twice as fast

a: Wake up as the parent for a day
b: Wake up as the youngest for a day

a: The car can fly, but only at walking pace
b: The car drives itself, but always takes the scenic route

a: A snowman that comes to life each winter
b: A sandcastle that survives every tide

a: Homework does itself but teaches you nothing
b: Homework takes hours but you remember it forever

a: A holiday where it rains chocolate
b: A holiday where the sea is warm as a bath

a: Your sibling must be nice to you on Mondays
b: You get the front seat, forever

a: Every bedtime story turns out to be true
b: Every good dream comes back with a sequel

a: A fridge that's always full of your favourites
b: A wardrobe that always has the perfect outfit

a: Movie nights that never get interrupted
b: Snacks that magically never run out

a: A swing set that gently launches you over the house
b: A slide that loops the loop

a: The whole family can teleport home from anywhere
b: The whole family never gets lost again

a: A robot that does every chore but hums constantly
b: No robot, but chores take half the time

a: A four-day week at 90% pay
b: A three-day week at 70% pay

a: Never do laundry again
b: Never do the dishes again

a: Inbox at zero forever, but no searching old emails
b: Keep every email, but the inbox is always 200 deep

a: Always know what people really think of you
b: Never wonder, but never be flattered either

a: A brilliant boss who never remembers your name
b: A mediocre boss who fights for you

a: Work from anywhere, but always on call
b: One fixed desk, but truly off at 5pm

a: A city-centre flat the size of a shoebox
b: A house an hour out with a real garden

a: Your salary doubles but your commute triples
b: Your salary stays but you work from your sofa

a: Retire at 45 with just enough
b: Retire at 65 with plenty

a: Find £200 in an old coat every month
b: A guaranteed lie-in every single Sunday

a: Coffee that tastes like the finest wine
b: Wine that tastes like the finest coffee

a: Always find a parking space
b: Always get a seat on the train

a: Perfect memory for names
b: Perfect memory for where you left things

a: A phone that never dies but is always a bit slow
b: A fast phone you charge twice a day

a: Free flights anywhere, middle seat guaranteed
b: Full price, but first class every time

a: Read minds, but only when people are bored
b: Be invisible, but only in meetings

a: Your rent is frozen for ten years
b: One completely mortgage-free year, starting now

a: Speak every language with a thick comedic accent
b: Speak two languages flawlessly

a: A job you love that pays just enough
b: A job you tolerate that pays double

a: Always know when someone's lying
b: Always be believed, even when you're wrong

a: Never be stuck in traffic again
b: Never wait on hold again

a: The gym is always empty when you arrive
b: Your favourite restaurant always has a table

a: Unlimited holiday, but always reachable
b: Four weeks off, completely unplugged

a: Meetings can never run over
b: Emails can never be marked urgent

a: Sleep perfectly, but only six hours
b: Sleep eight hours, occasionally badly

a: Win every argument, but people quietly resent it
b: Lose gracefully and stay beloved

a: A mentor who's harsh but always right
b: A cheerleader who's kind but vague

a: Move somewhere sunny, far from friends
b: Stay put under grey skies, everyone you love nearby

a: A promotion with more money and more stress
b: A sideways move with less of both

a: Always have the perfect comeback, an hour too late
b: Never need one, because you genuinely don't care

a: Unlimited coffee, but only instant
b: One perfect flat white a day

a: A clone who does your chores but takes half your luck
b: Do your own chores, keep all your luck

a: Know the exact day you'll feel happiest this year
b: Be completely surprised by it

a: Your bank warns you before every overspend
b: Your calendar deletes pointless meetings on its own

a: Never get a parking ticket again
b: Never catch a cold again

a: A weekend that lasts three days, but brutal Mondays
b: A normal weekend with gentle Mondays

a: Your savings grow 1% a day, untouchable for a year
b: A lump sum now, no growth

a: Always be ten minutes early, slightly anxious
b: Always be five minutes late, perfectly calm

a: A fridge that restocks the basics by itself
b: A washing machine that also folds

a: Be effortlessly tidy but a terrible cook
b: Be a brilliant cook but hopelessly messy

a: Hands slightly too small, forever
b: Hands slightly too big, forever

a: Ducks think you're their leader
b: Pigeons think you owe them money

a: Toast always lands butter-side up
b: It lands butter-side up, but only when no one's watching

a: Every song you hear gains a surprise kazoo solo
b: Every film you watch gains one unexplained goose

a: Your shadow is three seconds behind you
b: Your reflection always waves first

a: You sneeze glitter
b: You hiccup tiny soap bubbles

a: Animals understand you but find you hilarious
b: Animals understand you but pretend not to

a: Your phone autocorrects every word to 'moist'
b: Your phone answers all texts as a pirate

a: When you lie, a distant trumpet plays
b: When you're right, a small puff of confetti appears

a: You're immortal but always slightly peckish
b: You age normally but your left elbow ages backwards

a: Everything you touch becomes briefly warm
b: Everything you say briefly rhymes

a: A personal cloud that rains only compliments
b: A personal spotlight that follows you, slightly off-centre

a: You can teleport, but only to where you just were
b: You can fly, but only one foot off the ground

a: Your sneezes sound like a foghorn
b: Your laugh sounds like a dial-up modem

a: Bread is always warm when you hold it
b: Cheese says your name when you open the fridge

a: You can pause any music with a stern look
b: You can fast-forward awkward chats by ten seconds

a: Pigeons salute you on sight
b: Squirrels file formal complaints about you

a: Your hair changes colour with your mood
b: Your hair grows an inch whenever you lie

a: Objects do what you ask, but sigh first
b: Objects talk to you, but they're all exhausted

a: You can summon one sandwich a day, flavour random
b: You can summon any drink, but always lukewarm

a: Gravity is 10% weaker for you on Tuesdays
b: Time runs 10% slower for you on Sundays

a: A tiny orchestra plays when you enter a room
b: A tiny narrator describes your every move, fondly

a: You understand cats, but they only roast you
b: You understand dogs, but they overshare wildly

a: Your footsteps leave faint glittery prints
b: Your handshakes give a tiny spark of pure joy

a: Everything you draw comes alive, for one minute
b: Everything you write comes true, but spelled wrong

a: You turn invisible, but only while sneezing
b: You walk through walls, but only while singing

a: Wasps deeply respect you
b: Mosquitoes hold a personal grudge against you

a: Your future self texts you cryptic advice
b: Your past self leaves slightly out-of-date tips

a: You can breathe underwater, but only sparkling water
b: You can walk on clouds, but they're a bit sticky

a: Everyone hears your thoughts as gentle elevator music
b: Everyone faintly smells your mood, like a candle

a: You sprout tiny wings that flap when you're happy
b: You grow a tail that wags whenever you lie

a: Every mirror shows you in a tiny crown
b: Every photo of you has a faint, suspicious glow

a: You can teleport your keys straight to your hand
b: You can teleport one lost sock to its pair

a: Plants lean towards you and whisper encouragement
b: Clouds form little arrows pointing where to go

a: You hum and nearby machines hum in harmony
b: You sigh and nearby lights dim sympathetically

a: Your sneezes teleport you exactly one metre to the left
b: Your yawns make everyone nearby yawn, deliberately

a: You turn slightly translucent when embarrassed
b: You glow gently when proud

a: You can taste colours, but blue is always a let-down
b: You can hear shapes, but circles are far too loud

a: Spiders knit you tiny scarves
b: Bees leave you tiny handwritten thank-you notes

a: Your inner monologue has a laugh track
b: Your inner monologue is a calm documentary host

a: Pizza always arrives five minutes early but slightly wrong
b: Pizza is always exactly right but thirty minutes late

a: You can summon a gentle breeze on demand
b: You can summon one dramatic leaf to blow past

a: Every door you open creaks dramatically
b: Every light flickers ominously when you enter

a: You can rewind ten seconds, but everyone sees the glitch
b: You can skip ten seconds ahead, but lose the memory of it
